source_note: Manual walk of the iter-1 system-mode review findings. /opsx:address-reviews can't ingest internal review JSONs yet (blocked on orbit#10); this run executes the same lifecycle inline and persists the resolution log in the same shape /opsx:address-reviews would write.
executed_by: claude in-session, walk mode (per-finding decision on W1; trivial fix applied for W2; S1-S3 noted as informational/no-action)
pushback_verification: All 5 findings verified against current state at the moment of this walk. The iter-1 system review was emitted minutes before this address-reviews run; no artifacts changed in between. No stale findings suppressed.

resolutions.review_finding_title: Test suite covers ~30 of ~45 spec scenarios — load-bearing paths only
resolutions.action: Applied user decision: file a GitHub issue on las-sal/orbit-status. Filed as orbit-status#1 with a detailed breakdown of the ~15 untested scenarios per capability, plus rationale (v0.1 trade-off, plain bash test runner, mtemp fixtures) and suggested closure path (extend test runner; ~half day's work; no design decisions needed).

resolutions.review_finding_title: Stale line-count claims: docs say ~200/830 lines; actual is 1186
resolutions.action: Updated both files with the real count (1186) plus rationale explaining the size-growth (attention engine + 3-tier recommendation + multi-change focus ranking exceeded initial estimate). design.md framing kept the historical "~150-300 line target" as context and explained why current reality exceeds it; structure remains monolithic bash (single file, no build), so the growth is feature-scope not architectural drift.

resolutions.review_finding_title: Group 18 (4 tasks) unchecked but explicitly user-driven
resolutions.action: No action. Already documented in the task descriptions as the user-validation handoff boundary; /opsx:archive will surface the unchecked tasks as a warning at archive time, which is the expected behavior.

resolutions.review_finding_title: No external callers — cohesion trivially satisfied
resolutions.action: No action. Stand-alone leaf binary; cohesion check is vacuously satisfied. Caller-surface growth (hypothetical orbit-status-dashboard, CI integrations) would make this non-trivial in future versions.
No action in this walk. The recommendation surfaces in the next-step menu after this address-reviews run; user can opt for /opsx:review --as system --fresh as the iter-2 system pass before archive.

final_assessment: 5 findings walked. 2 resolved (line-count drift fixed in design.md + SKILL.md; test coverage gap tracked as orbit-status#1). 3 informational with no action. 1 escalated (recommend --fresh follow-up; surfaces in next-step menu). Ready to archive.
next_recommended: Either (a) proceed directly to /opsx:archive bootstrap-orbit-status-cli (all WARNINGs from the iter-1 system review are now resolved or tracked; SUGGESTIONs are informational), or (b) run /opsx:review --as system --fresh as a final anchoring-free pass before archive (per orbit#13's review-mode framework — iter-2 system mode in a clean-context subagent). (a) is the practical close-out; (b) is the rigorous close-out.
